title: [CI:DOCS] added docs for installing certificate authority
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat thanks much for the feedback - I should have read a bit more carefully before my commit/push/pull request.  And also thank you for the style/grammar/typos feedback as well.  I generally let material I write site for 24+ hours before proofreading because I catch very little otherwise (that did not happen here). Podman advancements have really been great and I am happy to contribute even if it's relatively minor/small stuff.  Also, very happy that my use case is supported.

Can you please squash your commits.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *carljmosca*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I need a bit of help in understanding what's probably obvious to most here on the failures above.  Looking at the files, they seem to have had the whitespace removed after running make format (had to move to my Fedora machine to get that working).  Mea culpa on not reading the requirements to contribute.
Running `git show --check 75c963aaa9ba07c3b506257a3d3702a22c015776` should show the whitespace errors.

Thank you @vrothberg - I should have said I saw that message in the log, and had already done that to see the trailing space...all good there.  I was referring to the mess I have made with the commits and merges in between and the lack of squashed commits.  The back-and-forth here and time spent switching to Fedora to get a good build so I could do the make validate, I've got way more time on overhead than the actual contributed docs.  Just wasn't expecting this for a "docs only" contribution but as I said above, that's on me for not becoming familiar with the process before starting.
Thanks for pushing this through, @carljmosca.  Your contribution is much appreciated!
Please squash your commits and remove whitespace.

The instructions in `podman-install-certificate-authority.md` assume a rootful machine.  Is it worth calling out the need to either switch to rootful or run `sudo` inside the machine? At least on macOS, the default configuration results in ssh-ing to the machine as the `core` user.
Excellent point @mitchellmebane  - my use case was running minikube on podman and root was required.  I am happy to update accordingly.
@carljmosca Please rebase and squash  your commits.
Sorry @rhatdan I thought I had already done the squash.

> 

I have added notes on rootless/becoming root.
@vrothberg is there anything needed from me on this now?
@TomSweeneyRedHat any reason this can't be merged?
